title: Effects_of_Acoustic_Excitation_on_a_Swirling_Diffusion_Flame
text:

Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

1.  **Mathematical equations:**

    *   B = (D/D_o)^2 = 0.563

2.  **Diagrams:**

    *   **Fig. 1 Experimental setup**
        *   *Components:* Laser-light sheet, CCD Camera, Nd:YLF Laser, Blockage disk, Fluidized bed particle generator, Rotameter, Acoustical filters, Ring blower, Nozzle, Loud speaker, To computer, Swirl generator, Settling chamber, Mesh screens, Honeycombs, Plenum chamber, Pressure regulator, C3H8 pressure tank, Function generator, Voltmeter, Oscilloscope, Power amplifier
           *Blockage disk dimensions* D, d

3.  **Key numbers, constants, and parameters:**

    *   Static pressure: 98.1 bars (ring blower)
    *   Ring blower power: 3.4 kW
    *   Test rig size: 600 x 400 mm^2
    *   Contraction ratio: 9
    *   Guide vane chord length: 60 mm
    *   Guide vane span length: 50 mm
    *   Pitch circle radius: 175 mm
    *   Vane angle: 0 deg to 70 deg (resolution of 0.5 deg)
    *   Blockage disk thickness: 1 mm
    *   Exit diameter D_o: 40 mm
    *   Blockage disk diameter D: 30 mm
    *   Blockage ratio B: 0.563
    *   Fuel composition: 95.0% C3H8, 3.5% C2H6, 1.5% C4H10
    *   Nozzle length: 300 mm
    *  Contraction ratio: 900
    *   Seamless stainless steel tube length: 600 mm
    *  Tube inner diameter: 5.0 mm
    *   Fuel jet fluid vented hole: d = 5.0 mm
    *   Loud speaker face diameter: 15 cm
    *   Voice coil electrical resistance: 6.6 ohms
    *   Moving mass of loud speaker: 22.07 g
    *   Frequency range: 10 Hz to 1200 Hz (resolution of 1 Hz)
    *   Maximum attainable excitation voltage: 25 V
    *   Particle diameter: 1 um
    *   Response frequency : 9000 Hz
    *   Argon-ion laser: 6 W
    *   Guide vanes NACA profile: NACA 0012

title: Effects_of_Acoustic_Excitation_on_a_Swirling_Diffusion_Flame
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   S = (Integral from -D/2 to D/2 of uwr dr) / (Dm/2 * Integral from -D/2 to D/2 of u r dr)
*   Re_a = u_a * D_h / v_a
*   Re_c = u_f * d / v_f
*   Dm = {D + D) / 2=35 mm)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **(a)  Shutter speed, 1/1000 s, Rec=2386**
    *Images of flames at Rea = 364, 1000, 1700. S=0.316, 0.541,0.596

    *X axis: r/D
    *Y axis: x/D

*   **(b) Dimensionless flame length in the domain of annular swirl strength**
    *   X-axis: Rea, range from approximately 0 to 1600
    *   Y-axis: L_0 / D, range from approximately 8 to 24
    *   Curves: Data points for different Rec values: 246, 857, 1469, 2386, 3609
    *  Regions: Weak swirling flame, Lifted flame, Transitional reattached flame
     *X axis: S range from approximately 0.342 to 0.531

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: Properties of LDV optical configurations**
    *   Headers: Wavelength (λ (nm)), Focal length of receiving and transmitting lens (mm), Beam intersection angle (deg), Diameter at beam crossing (mm), Fringe separation (μm), Number of fringe at measuring volume, Optical shift frequency (MHz), Dimension of measuring volume (δx, δy, δz (mm))
    *   Data:
        *   Green: 514.5, 500, 8.464, 0.234, 3.486, 67, 40, 0.235x0.234x3.170
        *   Blue: 488, 500, 8.464, 0.222, 3.306, 67, 40, 0.223x0.222x3.007

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   D\_m = 35 mm
*   Minimum resolution: 10 µm
*   Rea = 364, 1000, 1700
*   S = 0.316, 0.541, 0.596
*   Rec = 2386

*   **Fig. 4(b):**

*   **Fig. 5:**
    *   Axes:

            *   Flame mode C, Flame mode D, blowout

*   **Fig. 6:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Re
        *   y-axis: L/Lo
    *   Curves: Experimental data for different Re and St values:
        *   Re = 170, S = 0.150
        *   Re = 364, S = 0.316
        *   Re = 528, S = 0.426
        *   Re = 1000, S = 0.541
        *   Re = 1700, S = 0.596
        *   St = 0.167
        *   St = 0.175
        *   St = 0.243
        *   St = 0.278
        *   St = 0.328
    *   Shaded region indicates flame mode C, D.

*   NO_TABLES

**4. Diagrams:**

*   NO_DIAGRAMS

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   f = 180 Hz (resonant frequency)
*   Re = 2386 (specific Reynolds number, central jet exit)
*   S = 0.316, Re = 364 (parameters from Figure 4)
*   Strouhal number ~0.3 (near which velocity fluctuation amplitude increases)

title: Effects_of_Acoustic_Excitation_on_a_Swirling_Diffusion_Flame
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided image and text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   St numbers fall between 0.79 and 0.62
*   Lf/Lo value for S=0.316 and 0.541
*   Re = 2386, Re_g = 364, Re_c = 1000 or > 1000
*   Strouhal number = 0.257 describes a transition
*   Excit < 15 V

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   Fig 7(d)
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: t (ms)
        *   left y-axis: Eexc (volt)
        *   right y-axis: u (m/s)

        *   Eexc
        *   u
    *Key values: 0.5d, 0.6d

    *Fig 7(a)

        *   x-axis: r/D
        *   y-axis: x/D

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig 7(c): Elucidations of flow feature**
    *   Components and Labels:
        *   Surrounding air entrainment
        *   Swirl nozzle tip
        *   Radially moving mass
        *   Inner vortex ring about to exit
        *   Wrinkled flame surface
        *   Upper separation point
        *   Lower separation point
        *   Recirculation bubble
        *   Blockage disc
        *   Vortex ring just exit
    * *Alpha=120 degree and Alpha=280 degree*

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Strouhal number (transition): 0.257
*   Swirl strengths (S): 0.150, 0.316, 0.426, 0.541, 0.596
*   Reynolds number (Re): 246, 2182, 2598, 2696, 2124, 2178, 2386, 364, >1000
*   Stokes numbers: 0.02056, 0.00004
*   Laser wavelength (λ): 527 nm
*   Frame rate: 30 frames/s, 5000 frames/s, 3240 pictures/s
*   Shutter speed: 1/1000 s
* Acoustic frequency : 180 Hz

title: Effects_of_Acoustic_Excitation_on_a_Swirling_Diffusion_Flame
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 8]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   B = D^2 / Do^2
*   Dh = Do - D
*   Dm = (D + Do)/2
*   Rea (exit Reynolds number of annular flow, Eq. (2), dimensionless)
*   Rec (exit Reynolds number of central jet, Eq. (3), dimensionless)
*   S (Swirl number)
*   St_d (Central jet exit Strouhal number)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Fuel tube resonance frequency: 180 Hz
*   Central jet Strouhal number (flame mode C): 0.29
*   Alpha (angle): 280 deg
*   Strouhal number at the jet exit: 0.19
*   Reynolds number Re = 2386
*   Swirl strength close to 0.491 (Re = 728)

**Nomenclature (Symbols and Units):**

*   B: Blockage ratio, dimensionless
*   d: Diameter of circular blockage disk, mm
*   D: Diameter of central hole at circular blockage disk where the central jet goes out, mm
*   Dh: Hydraulic diameter of annular swirling jet at exit, mm
*   Dm: Mean diameter for the calculation of swirl number, mm
*   Do: Outer diameter of annular swirling jet at exit, mm
*   f: Excitation or forcing frequency, Hz
*   Lf: Length of flame during acoustic excitation, mm
*   Lo: Length of flame prior to acoustic excitation, mm
*   Eexc: Root mean square excitation voltage, V
*   r: Radial coordinate, mm
*   u: axial velocity component, m/s
*   ua: volumetric mean axial velocity of annular swirling jet at exit, m/s
*   uc: volumetric mean axial velocity of central jet at exit, m/s
*   t: time, ms
